description_long: This is a stout, wooden barrel with a classic, slightly bulging cylindrical shape and flat, circular ends. The body is constructed from dark brown wooden staves, each with a visible grain texture, and is reinforced by three thick, metallic hoops—one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the widest part of the barrel. The wood appears well-finished but not glossy, giving it a robust, utilitarian look suitable for storage or transport. The top and bottom surfaces are flat, making the barrel stable when standing upright and capable of supporting other objects. There are no visible handles, spigots, or markings, and the top is fully closed, indicating it is not currently open for filling or pouring. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops.
description: A sturdy, dark brown wooden barrel with three metallic hoops and a flat, closed top. The surface is matte and unmarked, giving it a classic, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly angled front, showing the flat top and the curvature of the wooden staves with all three metal hoops visible.
description_view_1: This view is similar to view 0 but rotated, highlighting the symmetry and the even spacing of the metal hoops around the barrel.
description_view_2: The barrel is shown from another rotated angle, maintaining the same features with the wood grain and metal bands clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view presents the barrel from yet another side, again emphasizing the uniformity of the wood and metal construction.
